Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to understand the DECLARE model and its constraints The model is given as a Python dictionary with keys init existence responded_existence precedence noncoexistence First recall DECLARE constraints types

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: init R must appear support 10 confidence 10 So every case must have R existence C must appear support 10 confidence 10 So every case must have C precedence C with activities R Precedence R C means R must come before C

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: responded_existence E with activities A Could be interpreted as RespondedExistence E A i e if E occurs then A must occur Or RespondedExistence A E i e if A occurs then E must occur Which is more plausible Lets see the intended process flow R A E P N C

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the responded_existence constraint is likely if A occurs then E must occur That aligns with the intended flow assignment leads to evaluation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: noncoexistence E with activities C Noncoexistence E C means E and C cannot both appear That would be contradictory because in the intended flow E occurs before C so they would coexist This constraint would be violated by any normal trace The main anomaly is the combination of existence C and noncoexistence E C

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possible reasons Misinterpretation of business requirements leading to contradictory rules Incremental changes in policies that were not reflected consistently Technical issues or incomplete data leading to erroneous DECLARE constraints Pressure to handle claims quickly resulting in constraints that allow skipping essential steps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Propose verification approaches Suggest SQL queries to check if the anomalies occur in practice Queries to find claims that were closed without any evaluation Queries to find traces where evaluation and closing coexist violating the models rules Queries to identify if evaluation steps always correspond with assigned adjusters
